behaviors.description: Renamed files appear in their own category and are not also counted as a deletion plus an addition.
behaviors.justification: The failure boundary is at real Git rename detection plus CLI parsing/output. Mocked Git responses can hide rename-threshold and status-format edge cases that only appear with an actual repository and git invocation.
behaviors.test_description: Create a temporary git repo, commit a file, rename it with `git mv`, commit again, run the CLI as a subprocess, and assert report shows 1 rename and 0 added/0 deleted for that change.

behaviors.description: Diffs that include binary files do not cause the tool to crash; binary files are counted in the output.
behaviors.justification: Binary diff handling depends on real Git diff behavior and actual binary file treatment. Mocking diff text can miss parser/subprocess failures triggered by real binary metadata/output.
behaviors.test_description: In a temp repo, commit then modify/add a binary file (e.g., random bytes), run the CLI subprocess, assert exit code is success, and verify binary-affected file is included in counts.

behaviors.description: Running the tool on a repository that has no commits yet produces an empty report rather than an error.
behaviors.justification: This behavior depends on real Git repository state (`HEAD` missing/unborn branch) and CLI error handling around subprocess exit/output. Mocking would likely bypass the exact failure mode.
behaviors.test_description: Initialize a new temp git repo with no commits, run the CLI subprocess, assert successful exit and an empty structured report instead of traceback/error output.

behaviors.description: The counts in the report match the actual change history of the repository being analyzed.
behaviors.justification: Correctness here is end-to-end against real repository history and Git semantics across added/deleted/renamed/modified states. Module-only tests can pass while real Git interactions produce different counts.
behaviors.test_description: Build a temp repo with a scripted sequence of commits containing known add/delete/rename/modify operations, run the CLI subprocess for the target range, and assert each reported category count matches the known ground truth.

behaviors.description: The output is identical whether the changes being analyzed are staged or unstaged.
behaviors.justification: Staged vs unstaged behavior relies on real index/worktree semantics in Git. This boundary is OS/filesystem + git plumbing and is easy to misrepresent with mocks.
behaviors.test_description: In a temp repo, make a change, capture CLI output with change unstaged; then stage the same change and run again; assert the structured outputs are identical.
